_comment: Manual corrections for known content errors in Mathpix SG exports. Applied to QuestionBlock.sg_text after parsing. Each patch is applied in order: sg_find/sg_replace for text substitutions, sg_append to add missing content at the end.

corrections.note: Part (d) solution truncated mid-sentence. The answer arctan(19.21913) is in the SG rubric tail but not the solution body.

sg_patches.sg_find: The angle between the path of the diver and the water is
sg_patches.sg_replace: The angle between the path of the diver and the water is $\arctan(19.21913) \approx 1.518$ or 1.519 radians.

corrections.note: Part (c) solution truncated after stating perpendicular slope is 1. Missing: f'(x)=1/(2sqrt(x))=1 => x=1/4, P=(1/4, 1/2).

sg_patches.sg_find: Thus a line perpendicular to the graph of $g$ has slope 1 .
sg_patches.sg_replace:
Thus a line perpendicular to the graph of $g$ has slope 1 .

$f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{1}{2\sqrt{x}}=1 \Rightarrow \sqrt{x}=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow x=\frac{1}{4}$

$f\!\left(\frac{1}{4}\right)=\sqrt{\frac{1}{4}}=\frac{1}{2}$

The coordinates of point $P$ are $\left(\frac{1}{4}, \frac{1}{2}\right)$.

corrections.note: Part (c) rubric missing entirely. Mathpix only captured two scoring notes but not the 5-point criteria for the separation of variables solution.

sg_patches.sg_find: Note: $\max 2 / 5[1-1-0-0-0]$ if no constant of integration
sg_patches.sg_replace:
$5:\left\{\begin{array}{l}1: \text { separates variables } \\ 1: \text { antiderivative of } \frac{1}{100-B} \text { term } \\ 1: \text { antiderivative of } \frac{1}{5} \text { term } \\ 1: \text { uses initial condition } \\ 1: \text { solves for } B(t)\end{array}\right.$

Note: $\max 2 / 5[1-1-0-0-0]$ if no constant of integration

corrections.note: Entire solution missing from Mathpix OCR â€” captured only as 'Note: This solution is valid for x > -1.' DE: dy/dx = y^2(2x+2), f(0) = -1.

sg_patches.sg_find: Note: This solution is valid for $x>-1$.
sg_patches.sg_replace:
(a) $f(0)=-1, \quad f^{\prime}(0)=(-1)^{2}(2 \cdot 0+2)=2$

$\lim_{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x)+1}{\sin x}=\frac{f^{\prime}(0)}{\cos 0}=\frac{2}{1}=2$

(b) $f(0)=-1, \quad \left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right|_{x=0}=(-1)^{2}(2)=2$

$f(0.25) \approx-1+2(0.25)=-0.5$

$\left.\frac{d y}{d x}\right|_{x=0.25}=(-0.5)^{2}(2(0.25)+2)=0.25 \cdot 2.5=0.625$

$f(0.5) \approx-0.5+0.625(0.25)=-0.34375$

(c) $\frac{d y}{y^{2}}=(2 x+2) d x$

$\int y^{-2} d y=\int(2 x+2) d x$

$-\frac{1}{y}=x^{2}+2 x+C$

$f(0)=-1: \quad 1=0+0+C \Rightarrow C=1$

$-\frac{1}{y}=x^{2}+2 x+1=(x+1)^{2}$

$f(x)=-\frac{1}{(x+1)^{2}}$

Note: This solution is valid for $x>-1$.

$2:\left\{\begin{array}{l}1: \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x)+1}{\sin x} \\ 1: \text { answer }\end{array}\right.$

$3:\left\{\begin{array}{l}1: \text { Euler's method with two steps } \\ 1: y(0.25) \\ 1: y(0.5)\end{array}\right.$

$5:\left\{\begin{array}{l}1: \text { separates variables } \\ 1: \text { antiderivatives } \\ 1: \text { constant of integration } \\ 1: \text { uses initial condition } \\ 1: \text { solves for } f(x)\end{array}\right.$
